discovery-readonly.title: Read-only discovery using task-approved targets
discovery-readonly.description: Allow a task to use its already documented/approved test targets for read-only DB/API and remote discovery.
allowed: Use task-documented approved test targets for read-only discovery.
Run read-only database/API checks and remote inspection commands needed by the active story.
Use runtime-only credentials/tokens supplied outside repository files.
denied: No configuration-changing remote commands, database writes, schema changes, deletes, production mutations, or batch apply.
No scanning unrelated hosts or expanding beyond task-documented targets.
No persistence of credentials, tokens, cookies, SNMP communities, private keys, passwords, or raw secret-bearing output.
evidenceRequired: Record approved targets used, read-only command/query/API intent, redacted result summary, and no-configuration-change statement.

readonly-db-api.title: Read-only database and backend API testing
readonly-db-api.description: Allow read-only test database access and backend API calls with runtime-only credentials/tokens.
allowed: Read-only queries against explicitly authorized test databases.
Backend API calls against test/local environments using runtime-provided authorization headers.
Evidence may record query intent, target environment, response shape, and redacted summaries.
denied: No database writes, schema changes, migrations, deletes, or production data mutation.
No persistence of database passwords, API tokens, headers, raw credential fields, or secret-bearing payloads in code, docs, config, logs, or evidence.
No use of test credentials as product defaults.
evidenceRequired: Record whether access was read-only, which non-secret target was used, and the redaction boundary.

frontend-test.title: Frontend visual testing
frontend-test.description: Allow Chrome DevTools driven frontend login, navigation, screenshots, and visual verification.
allowed: Use Chrome DevTools/browser tooling to log in with authorized test accounts.
Navigate local/test frontend pages, capture screenshots, inspect DOM/network, and verify visual behavior.
Restart or refresh local frontend dev server if the story validation requires it.
denied: No production account use unless explicitly included in the approval target.
No storage of passwords, cookies, tokens, or session secrets in repository files or evidence.
No global frontend style/API behavior changes outside the active task allowed paths.
evidenceRequired: Record tested URL, browser validation summary, and screenshot/evidence paths when relevant.

backend-restart.title: Backend runtime restart for testing
backend-restart.description: Allow taking over and restarting the local/test backend runtime for validation.
allowed: Stop stale local/test backend processes owned by the workspace validation flow.
Start or restart the configured backend runtime for the active task.
Run health checks and configured validation after restart.
denied: No production deployment, production process restart, or service-manager changes.
No destructive cleanup, data reset, migration, dependency upgrade, commit, or push.
No masking startup errors with fallback/mock responses.
evidenceRequired: Record restart command, process/port summary, health result, and any logs used for diagnosis.

remote-readonly.title: Remote read-only discovery
remote-readonly.description: Allow remote login to explicitly targeted servers/network devices for read-only discovery.
allowed: Remote login to the approved target only.
Run read-only inspection commands for logs, OS/platform clues, current network-device config display, and command-family discovery.
Use runtime-only SSH/API/device credentials supplied outside repository files.
denied: No configuration-changing commands, saves, commits, reloads, service restarts, package installs, file edits, or target data deletion.
No brute force, credential guessing, or scanning unrelated hosts.
No persistence of credentials, tokens, SNMP communities, private keys, cookies, or raw secret-bearing output.
evidenceRequired: Record target, read-only commands, exit status, redacted output summary, and explicit no-configuration-change statement.

controller-remote-readonly.title: Controller remote API read-only verification
controller-remote-readonly.description: Allow a task to call controller-provided remote access APIs for read-only verification on one explicit target.
allowed: Call controller /api/v1/remote/run or session APIs for approved read-only verification against the approved server, network device, SNMP, or WinRM target.
Use runtime-only credentials or tokens supplied outside repository files.
Record redacted request intent, target, result status, and verification summary.
denied: No configuration-changing commands, save/commit/reload, broad batch scope, guessed commands, or target expansion beyond the approved scope.
No persistence of controller auth tokens, SSH passwords, private keys, SNMP communities, WinRM passwords, cookies, or raw secret-bearing output.
No treating controller/API/remote failures as success.
evidenceRequired: Record target, controller endpoint, read-only command intent, response status, redacted output summary, and explicit no-configuration-change statement.

network-apply-syslog.title: Network-device syslog configuration apply
network-apply-syslog.description: Allow applying syslog target configuration to one explicitly approved network device.
allowed: Apply explicit reviewed syslog configuration commands to the approved network-device target.
Use the configured network-device execution library/protocol for the active task.
Verify resulting configuration with read-only display/show commands.
denied: No wildcard device scope or batch apply.
No guessed vendor commands, inferred listener address, or unreviewed template.
No SNMP trap apply, save/reboot, unrelated config changes, rollback, commit/push, or production route changes unless separately approved.
evidenceRequired: Record approved target, rendered commands with secrets redacted, precheck, apply result, verification output, and rollback/restore note if applicable.

server-apply-via-controller.title: Server remote apply through controller API
server-apply-via-controller.description: Allow server remote configuration only through controller-provided APIs for one explicit target/scope.
allowed: Call controller-provided APIs to perform approved server remote configuration for the approved target.
Record controller request intent, response result, and verification evidence with secrets redacted.
denied: No direct business-path SSH bypass for server apply.
No guessed commands, broad batch execution, production config changes, raw credential persistence, dependency changes, commit, or push.
No treating controller failure as success.
evidenceRequired: Record target, controller API endpoint, request intent, response status, and failure details if any.
